objective: Resolve exactly HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006 through bounded HCM-0.1 documentation and handoff-control-infrastructure repair, then request orchestration of a different fresh independent review without closing HCM-0.1.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

work_completed: Repaired the HCM-0.1 close gate so only a completed clean independent review closes the slice, and any actionable finding requires separate remediation followed by a different fresh review.

Adopted the authorized Phase 2/3 boundary: Phase 2 permits only fixed deterministic non-Resolution first-party review renderers; generic configured custom-kind projections and every Resolution-aware view remain after HCM-3.2/HCM-3.3.

Added stable schema_id and schema_version identity to the ArtifactInstanceDescriptor target contract and added explicit cursor fields plus deterministic initial/continuation semantics to both bounded SnapshotCapturePolicy windows.

Retained handoff-record.schema.json as immutable-history v1.0 compatibility truth and added a v1.1 new-record schema requiring snapshot_refs and semantic_refs; routed the current template and dispatch closeout to v1.1 without rewriting prior records.

Added a docs-scoped Draft 2020-12 validator that validates both record schemas, the current template, every immutable record, every ledger entry, unique IDs, canonical record/index parity, and a byte-for-byte in-memory ledger rebuild with nonzero failure behavior.
Kept all runtime/open program gates open, made no Rust/test/core-asset/installed-skill changes, and selected no shipped defaults.

deliverables.summary: Custom-kind and projection semantics now preserve the Phase 2 fixed-renderer versus Phase 3 generic/Resolution-aware engine boundary.
HCM-0.1 review closure and Phase 2/3 sequencing are explicit without weakening custom-kind registration, validation, or intake proof.
Artifact descriptor identity, projection phase ownership, and bounded-window cursor semantics are internally explicit.
Proof wording requires actual schema validation and a completed clean fresh review after any remediation.
The protocol routes historical v1.0 versus new v1.1 handoffs and requires the repository-owned Draft 2020-12 validator for closeout.
Current new-record schema requires snapshot_refs and semantic_refs while leaving the v1.0 historical schema untouched.
Deterministic Draft 2020-12 validation and ledger parity/rebuild utility.
Immutable remediation closeout that leaves HCM-0.1 review-required for a different fresh reviewer.

decisions.summary: Phase 2 is limited to fixed deterministic non-Resolution first-party human-review renderers; it receives no generic configured or Resolution-aware precursor projection engine.
decisions.authority_effect: Generic configured custom-kind projections and all Resolution-aware views begin only after the HCM-3.2 Context Resolution kernel and HCM-3.3 deterministic projection engine land.

decisions.summary: New handoffs use schema_version 1.1 while immutable historical records remain routed to the retained v1.0 schema.
decisions.authority_effect: Every new record now structurally requires explicit snapshot_refs and semantic_refs without invalidating or rewriting historical records.

findings.summary: Resolved in remediation: HCM-0.1 requires a completed independent review with no unresolved actionable findings, with separate remediation and a different later fresh review after findings.

Resolved in remediation: Phase 2 is limited to fixed deterministic non-Resolution first-party review renderers; generic configured custom-kind projections and every Resolution-aware view stay in Phase 3 after HCM-3.2/HCM-3.3.
Resolved in remediation: closeout uses a repository-owned Draft 2020-12 validator for handoffs and ledger entries; jq empty is explicitly syntax-only.
Resolved in remediation: historical v1.0 records retain their schema, while v1.1 is mandatory for new records and requires snapshot_refs plus semantic_refs.
Resolved in remediation: each bounded SnapshotCapturePolicy history window has an explicit cursor and deterministic null/continuation semantics.
Resolved in remediation: ArtifactInstanceDescriptor carries stable schema_id and schema_version fields with an explicit identity gate.

validation.evidence_ref: Version-routed Draft 2020-12 validation of both schemas, current template, full immutable record history, all ledger entries, unique IDs, exact record/index parity, and byte-for-byte in-memory rebuild.

validation.command: Bounded Python terminology and phase assertions across 02/04/05/06/08 plus v1.1 schema/template requirements
validation.evidence_ref: Phase 2 fixed-renderer and Phase 3 generic/Resolution-aware boundaries; completed-clean-review wording; descriptor identity; snapshot cursors; version routing.

validation.command: Relative Markdown target, portability, and archive-boundary assertions across the active control pack and handoff infrastructure
validation.evidence_ref: All relative Markdown targets resolve; no active link enters archived authority; no machine-absolute path appears in the checked active scope.
Every changed or untracked path remains under docs/specs/handbook-contract-membrane/.

pack_updates.reason: Make the Phase 2 fixed-renderer versus Phase 3 generic/Resolution-aware projection boundary explicit.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections
pack_updates.reason: Repair review closure and projection sequencing without weakening custom-kind registration/intake proof.
Add descriptor identity, Phase 3 projection ownership, and deterministic bounded-window cursor semantics.
Require actual schema proof and completed clean independent review.
Define version-compatible new-record enforcement and actual Draft 2020-12 closeout validation.
Require explicit snapshot and semantic reference containers for every new handoff.
Require v1.1 records and actual repository-owned schema validation.

next_session.objective: Select this remediation handoff and dispatch a different fresh independent reviewer to review the complete HCM-0.1 control pack and validation infrastructure; close HCM-0.1 only if that reviewer reports no unresolved actionable findings.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

allowed_scope: Orchestrate one different fresh, docs-only independent review of the complete HCM-0.1 control pack and handoff validation infrastructure.
Re-run the documented proof wall and report findings-first against live repo truth.
prohibited_scope: Do not use the remediation agent as the independent reviewer.
Do not close HCM-0.1 from this remediation handoff alone.
Do not implement Rust, modify tests/core assets/installed skills, select shipped defaults, or start later slices.
Do not rewrite immutable handoff records or prior dispatches.
acceptance_criteria: A different fresh independent reviewer evaluates all six remediated findings and the complete HCM-0.1 gate.
The reviewer reports no unresolved actionable findings before HCM-0.1 is closed.
The version-routed Draft 2020-12 validator and full ledger/history proof remain green.
stop_conditions: Keep HCM-0.1 open if any actionable finding remains.
Stop and dispatch bounded remediation rather than widening the review into implementation or later slices.

chat_summary: Resolved HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006 within the bounded docs/control-infrastructure scope. HCM-0.1 remains review-required; orchestration must dispatch a different fresh independent reviewer before any close decision.
